pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly writes to this file
export_result.sh checks this path as SCRIPT_PATH
export_result.sh checks this path as REPORT_PATH
Simulation in GMAT computes ~114 mins, placing it safely above the 30.0 limit, and verifier explicitly enforces this boundary.
Simulation in GMAT computes ~114 mins, placing it safely below the 400.0 limit, and verifier explicitly enforces this boundary.
Simulation in GMAT computes ~5012 mins, placing it safely above the 400.0 limit, and verifier explicitly enforces this boundary.

Simulation shows MAVEN has ~5012 mins vs MRO's ~114 mins.

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the GMAT orbital simulation synthetically models contact times between Perseverance (at Jezero Crater) and Mars orbiters MRO and MAVEN. GMAT execution validates that MAVEN achieves significantly more visibility time (~5012 mins) compared to MRO (~114 mins) over the 7-day period starting Jan 1, 2026. This explicitly confirms that MAVEN is the expected primary relay and both contact times fall within the verifier's expected thresholds. The expected script, spec, and report paths perfectly match the setup and export scripts.
